pattern: Sorted order lets two pointers converge from opposite ends, eliminating half the search space each step without extra storage.
hints: The array is sorted — if the current sum is too large, one pointer must move inward; if too small, the other must.
Place one pointer at the start and one at the end, then adjust based on whether their sum is above or below the target.
Move the left pointer right when the sum is too small, the right pointer left when too large; return 1-indexed positions when equal.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length, answer at positions 1 and 2
negative values summing to negative target
pair consists of two identical values
answer at the very last two positions
pair is not the first two elements despite sorted order
large run of equal values before the real pair

stress.description: sorted array of 30 k elements with the unique pair planted at random positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    # pick two distinct values that sum to target
    a = rng.randint(-500, 499)
    b = rng.randint(a + 1, 500)
    target = a + b
    # fill the rest ensuring no accidental second pair
    forbidden = set()
    forbidden.add(target - a)
    forbidden.add(target - b)
    pool = []
    while len(pool) < n - 2:
        v = rng.randint(-1000, 1000)
        if v not in forbidden:
            pool.append(v)
            forbidden.add(target - v)
    pool.append(a)
    pool.append(b)
    pool.sort()
    return [pool, target]
